title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple - Box Office Mojo
text: Box Office Mojo provides comprehensive box office data for 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple. The film earned an estimated $15 million over its four-day opening weekend at the MLK holiday frame, consisting of $13 million for the traditional three-day weekend. Domestic (US and Canada) total: $25.1 million. International box office: $33.4 million. Worldwide total: $58.5 million. Production budget: $63 million. The film opened at 3,400 theaters across North America. Worldwide opening weekend: $31.2 million across 61 territories. The UK-Ireland led international markets with $4.6 million, followed by Mexico with $1.5 million, and Australia with $1 million. The film's performance represents a significant underperformance compared to its predecessor, 28 Years Later, which opened to $30 million domestically in June 2025 and eventually reached $151.3 million globally. With a higher production budget than the predecessor, the financial trajectory raises profitability concerns for Sony, as the film would need to achieve approximately $157.5 million worldwide to break even using standard 2.5x multiplier industry benchmarks.
